mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.3% in April 2014 reflects ongoing post-crisis labor recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero combined with sustained low inflation signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premium and supports valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 236.254 in April 2014
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or correlation between FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction term and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strengthening residential construction demand and forward-looking economic optimism, which cascades into multiplier effects across employment and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 946.0 thousand units in March 2014
regime_hint: housing recovery phase in post-crisis expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 52% of instances when HOUST rises > 5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side productivity gains, signaling robust corporate profitability and positive earnings revisions that drive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.7435 in April 2014 reflects post-crisis manufacturing recovery
regime_hint: any regime with positive industrial production momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 50% of instances when INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth combined with rising payroll employment signals strengthening consumer purchasing power and demand momentum, which jointly drive corporate revenue growth and equity valuations upward over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.15%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 434571.0 million and PAYEMS at 138252.0 thousand in April 2014
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with labor market strength
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or interaction term (RSAFS × PAYEMS) shows rank correlation < 0.04 with forward returns over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by CPI combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL growth subdued, making this trigger inactive in May 2014
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth combined with declining unemployment rate signals tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and corporate labor costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth > 0.6% AND UNRATE declines > 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138252.0 and UNRATE at 6.3% in April 2014 show moderate labor market tightening
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with accelerating labor market tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 45% of triggered instances, or interaction term shows rank correlation < 0.05 with forward returns over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly combined with sustained retail sales momentum signals robust aggregate demand that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% annualized AND RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15946.6 billion chained 2017 dollars in Q1 2014 reflects ongoing expansion
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term (GDPC1 × RSAFS) shows rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.
